claim: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol enables agents to negotiate and communicate directly with each other, not just access tools
actionable: Use the A2A protocol to enable multi-agent orchestration where agents discover and communicate with peer agents using HTTP, SSE, and JSON-RPC standards

claim: Agent discovery and marketplace concepts are fundamental to future agent systems—agents will select needed agents from a registry
actionable: Design multi-agent systems with registries or APIs where agents can discover peer agents matching their task requirements

claim: A2A protocol intentionally complements rather than replaces MCP (Model Context Protocol)
actionable: Use MCP for agent-to-tool communication and A2A for agent-to-agent communication in hierarchical systems

claim: Anthropic's absence from A2A partner list raises concerns about fragmented agent protocols if they extend MCP separately
actionable: Monitor protocol standardization efforts across major labs to avoid building on incompatible agent communication frameworks
